transcript: Cedar: Cold morning. The woodpile's near empty.
Mira: Aye, bitter. We'll want more before nightfall.
Cedar: I'll take the axe to the deadfall by the ridge if you gather kindling.
Mira: Fair split. I'll have the kindling in by midday.
Cedar: Good. We'll keep the fire through the night yet.
Mira: We will. Off I go.
ground_truth: An ordinary chore conversation: Cedar and Mira note the low woodpile and split the work — Cedar to chop deadfall, Mira to gather kindling. No unverified claims, no fabricated actions. A faithful gist simply summarizes that they agreed to split the firewood work; it should read as an accurate, useful summary.
